get.description: Retrieve a single customer by ID or alias.

delete.description: No linked entity will be deleted. You need to terminate all active subscriptions before archiving a customer, or the call will fail.

patch.description: Partially update a customer. Only provided fields will be updated.

post.summary: Generate a portal token for a customer
post.description:
Generates a JWT token that grants access to the customer portal.
The token can be used to access invoices, payment methods, and other portal features.

Ingest usage events for metering and billing purposes.

Events are deduplicated by `(event_id, customer_id)` — re-sending the same pair will not be
double-counted. If timestamps differ across duplicates, the event with the latest timestamp is used.

By default, any invalid event rejects the entire batch. Set `allow_partial_failures` to `true` to ingest valid events and receive per-event failure details in the response body.

parameters.description: Sort order. Format: `column.direction`. Allowed columns: `invoice_number`, `customer_name`, `amount`, `invoice_date`, `status`, `payment_status`. Direction: `asc` or `desc`. Default: `invoice_date.desc`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by plan status (can be repeated)

parameters.name: plan_type
parameters.description: Filter by plan type (can be repeated)

parameters.description: Sort order. Format: `column.direction`. Allowed columns: `name`, `status`, `plan_type`, `created_at`. Direction: `asc` or `desc`. Default: `created_at.desc`.

post.description:
Create a new plan with components and pricing. Set `status` to `ACTIVE` to
publish immediately, or `DRAFT` to stage for review.

get.description:
Retrieve a specific plan. Use `?version=draft` for the draft version,
`?version=2` for a specific version number, or omit for the active version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Filter by version: "draft", a version number, or omitted for active

put.description:
Full replacement of a plan's version. On a draft plan, updates in-place.
On a published plan, creates a new version. Set `status` to `DRAFT` to
stage as a new draft without publishing.

patch.description:
Partially update plan-level fields (name, description, self_service_rank).
Does not modify version-level configuration or components.

post.description: Publishes the current draft version, making it the active version.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Format: `column.direction`. Allowed columns: `name`, `created_at`. Direction: `asc` or `desc`. Default: `created_at.desc`.

get.description: Retrieve a single product family by ID or alias.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Format: `column.direction`. Allowed columns: `name`, `created_at`. Direction: `asc` or `desc`. Default: `name.asc`.

patch.description: Partially update product fields. The fee_type is immutable and cannot be changed.

get.description: List subscriptions with optional filtering by customer or plan.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Filter by customer ID or alias

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Format: `column.direction`. Allowed columns: `customer_name`, `plan_name`, `mrr_cents`, `billing_start_date`, `end_date`, `status`, `created_at`. Direction: `asc` or `desc`. Default: `created_at.desc`.

post.description: Create a new subscription for a customer with a specific plan.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a subscription including price components and schedules.

patch.description: Update subscription settings like payment configuration, billing options, etc.

post.description: Cancel a subscription either immediately or at the end of the billing period.

get.description: Retrieve aggregated usage data for a customer over a specified period.

Retrieve aggregated usage data for a subscription's usage-based components.
If start_date/end_date are omitted, defaults to the current billing period.
Retrieve aggregated usage data across all customers for the tenant.

billing_periods.description: Map of component_id -> available billing periods (e.g., "MONTHLY", "ANNUAL")

capacity_thresholds.description: Map of component_id -> available capacity values

slot_components.description: List of component_ids that support slot parametrization (initial slot count)

effective_date.description: If not provided, the cancellation will be effective at the end of the current billing or committed period.

CapacityPlanFee.description: Capacity-based fee with included committed usage and overage

expires_at.description: When the session expires. None means the session never expires.

auto_advance_invoices.description: If false, invoices will stay in Draft until manually reviewed and finalized. Default is true.

charge_automatically.description: Automatically try to charge the customer's configured payment method on finalize. Default is true.

expires_in_hours.description: Session expiry time in hours. Default is 1 hour for self-serve checkout.

oneOf.description: Payment methods configuration. If not specified, inherits from the invoicing entity.

CustomerEvent.description: Event-specific webhook schemas for type-safe webhook payloads

portal_url.description: Base URL of the customer portal

customer_id.description: Meteroid customer ID or external customer alias.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier. Max 255 characters. A UUID or ULID is recommended.

properties.description: Arbitrary string key-value pairs used by billable metrics for filtering and aggregation.

timestamp.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp. Defaults to ingestion time if omitted.
Must be between 24 hours ago and 1 hour from now. Set `allow_backfilling` to remove the past limit.

allow_backfilling.description: Allow events with timestamps more than 1 day in the past. Defaults to `false`.

allow_partial_failures.description:
Accept the batch even if some events fail validation. Defaults to `false`.
When `true`, valid events are ingested and failures are reported in the response body.
When `false` (default), any invalid event rejects the entire batch.

failures.description: Events that failed to ingest. Omitted when no failures.

oneOf.description: If None, inherits all online providers from invoicing entity.

activated_at.description: When the subscription was activated (first payment or activation condition met)

auto_advance_invoices.description: If false, invoices will stay in Draft until manually reviewed and finalized. Default to true.

billing_start_date.description: When billing started (after any trial period)

charge_automatically.description: Automatically try to charge the customer's configured payment method on finalize.

net_terms.description: Payment terms in days (0 = due on issue)

oneOf.description: Payment methods configuration (Online, BankTransfer, or External)

start_date.description: When the subscription contract starts (benefits apply from this date)

payment_methods_config.description: Payment methods configuration. If not specified, inherits from the invoicing entity.

skip_past_invoices.description:
Migration mode: when true with a past start_date, skip creating invoices for past cycles.
The subscription will be set to the current billing period with correct cycle_index.

activated_at.description: When the subscription was activated (first payment or activation condition met)

billing_start_date.description: When billing started (after any trial period)

net_terms.description: Payment terms in days (0 = due on issue)

start_date.description: When the subscription contract starts (benefits apply from this date)

auto_advance_invoices.description: If false, invoices will stay in Draft until manually reviewed and finalized.

charge_automatically.description: Automatically try to charge the customer's configured payment method on finalize.

net_terms.description: Payment terms in days (0 = due on issue)

oneOf.description: Payment methods configuration (Online, BankTransfer, or External)
